keyPointsSummary: Same form for all subjects — no exceptions!
Had never changes. I had, he had, she had, they had — always the same!
Use for physical possession: I had a bike, She had a laptop
Use for relationships: He had a girlfriend, We had a teacher
Use for abstract things: They had a plan, I had an idea
Had = था/थी/थे (past possession). Use it whenever something existed in the past.
Used to show 'earlier past' — something that happened before another past event
Common V3 forms: eaten, gone, finished, done, written, seen, spoken
Had + V3 = something that was already done before another past event.
